proposals.statement: The combined Paramount Skydance / WBD entity is reported to carry roughly $54-57.5 billion of new debt commitments to fund the all-cash acquisition, with post-deal leverage of approximately 6-6.5x EBITDA.

proposals.quote: Banks are lining up roughly $54-57.5 billion in debt commitments for what would be the largest media merger in history, with post-deal leverage hitting 6-6.5x EBITDA.

proposals.statement: The combined Paramount-WBD entity is expected to carry between $79 billion and $87 billion in total debt after closing, per independent reporting.

proposals.statement: In an April 2026 restructuring, the debt deals reduced Paramount's aggregate long-term debt commitments from $54 billion to $49 billion, and reduced previously disclosed commitments for a $3.5 billion revolving credit facility to zero.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Restructures Debt Financing for Warner Bros. Discovery Deal

proposals.quote: The debt deals will reduce Paramount’s aggregate long-term debt commitments from $54 billion to $49 billion. In addition, Paramount’s previously disclosed commitments for a $3.5 billion revolving credit facility have been reduced to zero.

proposals.statement: Paramount amended its existing senior unsecured revolving credit facility to increase committed liquidity from $3.5 billion to $5 billion in advance of the closing of the merger.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Restructures Debt Financing for Warner Bros. Discovery Deal

proposals.quote: The company also amended its existing senior unsecured revolving credit facility to increase committed liquidity from $3.5 billion to $5 billion in advance of the closing of the merger.

proposals.statement: Paramount syndicated the bridge loan commitments across 18 banks to reduce Citi, BofA and Apollo's exposure, and entered permanent financing for the combined capital structure including a $5 billion Term Loan A and a $5 billion new revolver.

source.title: Paramount Skydance Restructures Debt Financing for Warner Bros. Discovery Deal

proposals.quote: Through the deals, Paramount syndicated the bridge loan commitments to reduce Citi, BofA and Apollo’s exposure and spread the total amount across 18 banks.

proposals.quote: These agreements include leverage covenants and a path to unsecured status if investment-grade ratings are achieved.

proposals.statement: Paramount Skydance management (David Ellison) characterizes post-close leverage as near 4.3x including the $6 billion of synergies and a post-transaction debt level of about $79 billion, and intends to return to investment grade within three years.

proposals.quote: He describes expected synergies of $6 billion , post-close leverage near 4.3x (including the synergies), and a post-transaction debt level he characterizes as about $79 billion .

source.title: Paramount Enhances Its Superior $30 Per Share All-Cash Offer for WBD (8-K Ex-99.1)

proposals.quote: Larry Ellison has provided an irrevocable personal guarantee, now of $43.3 billion, on the equity financing for our Revised Offer, the cash enhancements to certainty and any damages claims against Paramount.

proposals.statement: Paramount will fund the $2.8 billion termination fee due to Netflix, eliminate WBD's potential $1.5 billion debt-exchange financing cost (backstopped, without reduction to the separate $5.8 billion reverse termination fee), and its debt financing sources are prepared to extend WBD's existing $15 billion bridge loan if needed.

source.title: Paramount Enhances Its Superior $30 Per Share All-Cash Offer for WBD (8-K Ex-99.1)

proposals.quote: Bridge loan refinancing : If WBD’s financing sources will not extend the maturity of WBD’s existing $15 billion bridge loan, Paramount’s debt financing sources are fully prepared to do so (with any incremental costs covered by Paramount).
proposals.locator: 8-K Ex-99.1, Feb 10, 2026 (enhanced offer provisions, bullet list)

proposals.statement: On a standalone basis, as of December 31, 2025 WBD had $4,566 million of cash and cash equivalents on hand.
proposals.quote: As of December 31, 2025, we had $4,566 million of cash and cash equivalents on hand.
proposals.locator: FY2025 10-K, MD&A Liquidity and Capital Resources (p. 50)

proposals.statement: WBD's standalone scheduled long-term debt repayments as of December 31, 2025 total approximately $32.7 billion (2026: $139M; 2027: $16,483M; 2028: $1,409M; 2029: $2,274M; 2030: $1,354M; thereafter: $11,186M), with $16.5 billion concentrated in the 2027 maturity tower.

On the WBD side, a JPMorgan-led bank group refinanced WBD's $15 billion bridge loan with longer-term debt, with WBD's junk credit grade (on the higher end for junk-rated borrowers) and scarce leveraged-loan supply generating more than $30 billion of investor orders.

source.title: Paramount Skydance And Warner Bros. Discovery Face One Of The Year's Biggest Debt Market Tests

proposals.quote: On the Warner Bros. side, a JPMorgan Chase-led bank group seized on red-hot demand for leveraged loans to save hundreds of millions of dollars in interest costs by refinancing a $15 billion bridge loan with longer-term debt.

proposals.statement: S&P treated David Ellison's verbal pledge to keep Paramount's leverage in line with forecasts as a tacit promise to inject additional capital if needed, and pushed for that commitment to be made public after deal skeptics labeled post-merger leverage levels 'frightening.'

source.title: Paramount Skydance And Warner Bros. Discovery Face One Of The Year's Biggest Debt Market Tests

proposals.quote: Chief Executive Officer David Ellison pledged that his family would do what it takes to keep Paramount’s leverage in line with forecasts, with S&P treating the verbal pledge as a tacit promise to inject additional capital if needed.

proposals.statement: There is a material gap between the interested-party leverage framing and independent reporting: management cites post-close leverage near 4.3x (fin_13), but independent reporting puts pro-forma leverage at 6-6.5x EBITDA (fin_1) and S&P does not expect leverage below its 4.25x threshold until 2029 (fin_12). The ~2x gap is largely explained by management presenting leverage net of the full $6 billion of not-yet-realized synergies (fin_6, fin_13), which is exactly the execution risk S&P flags.

Both major agencies that rated the deal cut Paramount to junk: S&P will take PSKY to 'BB' on close (fin_12) and Fitch had already downgraded Paramount to junk status (fin_13 references Fitch's junk downgrade), confirming the combined entity will fund and close as a non-investment-grade borrower despite management's stated three-year investment-grade ambition (fin_13).

The deleveraging path rests on synergy execution against a structurally declining base: the plan targets net debt/adjusted EBITDA below 3.75x by FY2028 and 3.0x by FY2029 (fin_5), but that requires realizing over $6 billion of synergies (fin_6) on top of ~$79-87B of total debt (fin_2) to acquire WBD, whose largest segment (Global Linear Networks) is in secular decline — the precise risk S&P cites in keeping leverage above 4.25x until 2029 (fin_12) and in treating the Ellison family pledge as a backstop to inject capital if forecasts slip (fin_19).

Combined-entity liquidity is supported by a $5.0 billion upsized unsecured revolver (fin_8, fin_10) plus WBD's standalone ~$4.6 billion cash balance (fin_16), but the near-term refinancing wall is concentrated in WBD's ~$16.5 billion of 2027 maturities (fin_17), meaning the combined company must access debt markets at junk spreads (fin_12) to term those out — a dependency the deal partly pre-empted via WBD's $15 billion bridge refinancing (fin_18).
